problem_spec.summary: Determine a uniquely forced affine form from a Pexider-Jensen equation plus boundedness and algebraic anchor constraints, then compute an encoded integer output.
givens: Pexider-Jensen equation $f((x+y)/2)=(g(x)+h(y))/2$ for all real x,y.
Regularity guard: f is bounded above on [0,1].
Additional constraint atom: f is strictly increasing on [0,\infty).
ask: Compute the integer N defined by reducing $f(2026)=p/q$ and taking $N\equiv p+q\pmod{1000}$, $0\le N<1000$.

parameter(s).name: regularity_guard
parameter(s).purpose: Select which constraint eliminates pathological solutions.

invariant(s).name: translation_invariance
invariant(s).statement: The Pexider-Jensen equation forces the invariant difference g(x)-h(x)=constant, reducing to the Jensen midpoint equation for f.

invariant(s).name: rational_scaling
invariant(s).statement: After forcing additivity for F(x)=f(x)-f(0), one gets F(qx)=qF(x) for all rational q.

invariant(s).name: interface_quantity_T
invariant(s).statement: The slope parameter T=F(1)=f(1)-f(0) determines f on \mathbb{Q} and, under regularity, on \mathbb{R}.

invariant(s).name: constraint_atoms_rigidity
invariant(s).statement: Boundedness on an interval forces continuity at 0 for additive solutions, hence linearity; monotonicity selects the correct branch among finitely many slopes.

essential_assumption(s): \mathbb{R} is closed under required midpoint and addition operations.
Boundedness on [0,1] holds to regularize Jensen/additive solutions.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Pexider reduction gives the Jensen midpoint equation, which forces additivity for F=f-f(0). Boundedness implies continuity at 0, hence f is affine with uniquely determined slope from the anchor constraints, and monotonicity eliminates the remaining branch.

technique(s).name: plug_special_values
technique(s).purpose: obtain basepoint relations such as f(0) and links among g(0),h(0).

technique(s).name: swap_variables
technique(s).purpose: derive symmetry/cancellation identities leading to g-h constant.

technique(s).name: bounding_trap
technique(s).purpose: force continuity of an additive function by shrinking arguments.

technique(s).name: classify_piecewise
technique(s).purpose: split into slope cases and eliminate incompatible ones using monotonicity.

technique(s).name: force_additivity
technique(s).purpose: reduce to additive_Cauchy for the normalized function F.

theorem(s).name: Jensen_type
theorem(s).role: midpoint Jensen equation reduces to additivity for F=f-f(0); boundedness regularizes.

theorem(s).name: Pexider_type
theorem(s).role: swapping variables forces g-h constant and collapses Pexider-Jensen to Jensen.

critical_trick(s): Extract the invariant offset k=g-h by swapping variables, then eliminate it to obtain the Jensen midpoint equation for f.
Normalize by F=f-f(0) to force additivity, then apply boundedness to obtain continuity and linearity.
Use the algebraic anchor constraints to solve for the slope and select the correct branch via monotonicity.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: symbolic verification of the functional identity under the derived affine forms and solving the resulting parameter constraints.

library(s).name: random
library(s).reason: randomized numerical testing of the derived closed forms on sampled real inputs.

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason: Use SymPy to (i) verify that the derived affine form satisfies the Pexider-Jensen equation identically and (ii) solve the anchor constraint system to confirm the only possible slopes are {1,-5} with k=\pm2, then apply monotonicity to select c=1.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: Randomly sample real pairs (x,y) to numerically confirm $f((x+y)/2)=(g(x)+h(y))/2$ for the chosen representative (k=2) and compute N from the reduced fraction of f(2026).
